main_paper.raw: {"methods":["Augmentation-conditioning: conditioning a frozen, pretrained diffusion model on an augmented real image and text prompt to generate synthetic training images"],"problems":["Improving the diversity of generated images for use as effective synthetic training data for image classification","Generating synthetic images that are both realistic and diverse, especially for long-tail and few-shot classification tasks","Reducing the computational cost of generating effective synthetic training data without fine-tuning diffusion models"],"datasets":["ImageNet Long-Tailed","Four standard few-shot image classification benchmarks (exact names not specified in the provided text)"],"metrics":["Classification accuracy"],"results":[{"metric":"Classification accuracy on ImageNet Long-Tailed","value":"Outperforms prior work while using 1/3.5k less synthetic images"},{"metric":"Classification accuracy on four few-shot benchmarks","value":"Surpasses state-of-the-art, with remarkable gains in extreme few-shot regimes"}],"novelty_claims":["Proposes augmentation-conditioning, a method that uses data augmentations of real images as conditioning information for image generation with a frozen, pretrained diffusion model, without any generative model fine-tuning.","Demonstrates that augmentation-conditioning yields more visually diverse and in-domain synthetic images, improving downstream classification performance over previous methods.","Achieves state-of-the-art results on long-tail and few-shot classification benchmarks without the need for computationally expensive diffusion model training or fine-tuning."]}

methods: Augmentation-conditioning: conditioning a frozen, pretrained diffusion model on an augmented real image and text prompt to generate synthetic training images
problems: Improving the diversity of generated images for use as effective synthetic training data for image classification
Generating synthetic images that are both realistic and diverse, especially for long-tail and few-shot classification tasks
Reducing the computational cost of generating effective synthetic training data without fine-tuning diffusion models
datasets: Four standard few-shot image classification benchmarks (exact names not specified in the provided text)

results.value: Outperforms prior work while using 1/3.5k less synthetic images

results.metric: Classification accuracy on four few-shot benchmarks
results.value: Surpasses state-of-the-art, with remarkable gains in extreme few-shot regimes

novelty_claims: Proposes augmentation-conditioning, a method that uses data augmentations of real images as conditioning information for image generation with a frozen, pretrained diffusion model, without any generative model fine-tuning.
Demonstrates that augmentation-conditioning yields more visually diverse and in-domain synthetic images, improving downstream classification performance over previous methods.
Achieves state-of-the-art results on long-tail and few-shot classification benchmarks without the need for computationally expensive diffusion model training or fine-tuning.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Using synthetic images generated by state-of-the-art text-to-image models (GLIDE) for image recognition tasks","Evaluating synthetic data in zero-shot and few-shot classification settings","Strategies for increasing synthetic data diversity via diversified text prompts","Measuring correlation of text and synthesized data with CLIP features to enhance data reliability","Using real images as guidance in the generation process to reduce domain gap in few-shot settings","Large-scale model pre-training with synthetic data for transfer learning","Combining synthetic data with real data (e.g., ImageNet) for model initialization"],"problems":["Assessing the applicability of synthetic images from generative models for image recognition tasks","Improving classification models in data-scarce settings (zero-shot and few-shot) using synthetic data","Evaluating the effectiveness of synthetic data for large-scale model pre-training and transfer learning","Addressing the domain gap between synthetic and real data in recognition tasks"],"datasets":["17 diverse image recognition datasets (including EuroSAT)","ImageNet"],"metrics":["Top-1 accuracy"],"results":[{"metric":"Average top-1 accuracy improvement (zero-shot setting)","value":"4.31%"},{"metric":"Top-1 accuracy improvement on EuroSAT (zero-shot setting)","value":"17.86%"}],"novelty_claims":["First extensive study of state-of-the-art text-to-image generation models (GLIDE) for image recognition tasks","Demonstrates the effectiveness and limitations of synthetic data from text-to-image models for zero-shot, few-shot, and transfer learning settings","Proposes strategies to increase synthetic data diversity and reliability for recognition tasks","Shows that synthetic data can outperform ImageNet pre-training in transfer learning, especially for ViT-based backbones","Introduces the use of real images as guidance in synthetic data generation to reduce domain gap in few-shot settings"]}

methods: Using synthetic images generated by state-of-the-art text-to-image models (GLIDE) for image recognition tasks
Evaluating synthetic data in zero-shot and few-shot classification settings
Strategies for increasing synthetic data diversity via diversified text prompts
Measuring correlation of text and synthesized data with CLIP features to enhance data reliability
Using real images as guidance in the generation process to reduce domain gap in few-shot settings

Combining synthetic data with real data (e.g., ImageNet) for model initialization
problems: Assessing the applicability of synthetic images from generative models for image recognition tasks
Improving classification models in data-scarce settings (zero-shot and few-shot) using synthetic data
Evaluating the effectiveness of synthetic data for large-scale model pre-training and transfer learning
Addressing the domain gap between synthetic and real data in recognition tasks

results.metric: Top-1 accuracy improvement on EuroSAT (zero-shot setting)

novelty_claims: First extensive study of state-of-the-art text-to-image generation models (GLIDE) for image recognition tasks
Demonstrates the effectiveness and limitations of synthetic data from text-to-image models for zero-shot, few-shot, and transfer learning settings
Proposes strategies to increase synthetic data diversity and reliability for recognition tasks
Shows that synthetic data can outperform ImageNet pre-training in transfer learning, especially for ViT-based backbones
Introduces the use of real images as guidance in synthetic data generation to reduce domain gap in few-shot settings

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Fine-tuning large-scale text-to-image diffusion models (specifically Imagen) to produce class-conditional generative models on ImageNet","Using generated synthetic images to augment the ImageNet training set for classification tasks","Careful selection of guidance weights and sampling parameters to improve sample alignment and diversity"],"problems":["Can generative models (specifically diffusion models) produce synthetic data of sufficient quality and diversity to improve discriminative tasks such as ImageNet classification?","Does augmenting real training data with synthetic data from diffusion models improve classification accuracy over strong baselines?"],"datasets":["ImageNet"],"metrics":["Frechet Inception Distance (FID)","Inception Score (IS)","Classification Accuracy Score (CAS)"],"results":[{"metric":"FID (256x256)","value":"1.76"},{"metric":"Inception Score (256x256)","value":"239"},{"metric":"Classification Accuracy Score (256x256 synthetic samples)","value":"64.96"},{"metric":"Classification Accuracy Score (1024x1024 synthetic samples)","value":"69.24"}],"novelty_claims":["First to show that large-scale text-to-image diffusion models, when fine-tuned, can produce state-of-the-art class-conditional generative models on ImageNet.","Demonstrates that synthetic data from these models can set new state-of-the-art in Classification Accuracy Score when used to train classifiers evaluated on real data.","Shows that augmenting ImageNet with synthetic data from diffusion models significantly improves classification accuracy over strong ResNet and Vision Transformer baselines."]}

methods: Fine-tuning large-scale text-to-image diffusion models (specifically Imagen) to produce class-conditional generative models on ImageNet
Using generated synthetic images to augment the ImageNet training set for classification tasks
Careful selection of guidance weights and sampling parameters to improve sample alignment and diversity
problems: Can generative models (specifically diffusion models) produce synthetic data of sufficient quality and diversity to improve discriminative tasks such as ImageNet classification?
Does augmenting real training data with synthetic data from diffusion models improve classification accuracy over strong baselines?
novelty_claims: First to show that large-scale text-to-image diffusion models, when fine-tuned, can produce state-of-the-art class-conditional generative models on ImageNet.
Demonstrates that synthetic data from these models can set new state-of-the-art in Classification Accuracy Score when used to train classifiers evaluated on real data.
Shows that augmenting ImageNet with synthetic data from diffusion models significantly improves classification accuracy over strong ResNet and Vision Transformer baselines.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["TTIDA (Text-to-Text-to-Image Data Augmentation): a pipeline that first uses a fine-tuned text-to-text (T2T) model (e.g., GPT-2, T5) to generate detailed object descriptions from label text, then uses a text-to-image (T2I) model (e.g., GLIDE) to generate photo-realistic images conditioned on these descriptions, which are used to augment the original dataset."],"problems":["Limited diversity and controllability in traditional and GAN-based data augmentation methods for vision tasks.","Data scarcity and imbalance in labeled datasets for computer vision tasks.","Lack of flexible and domain-agnostic data augmentation approaches for various scenarios (few-shot, long-tail, adversarial, cross-domain)."],"datasets":["CIFAR","Office","MS COCO"],"metrics":["Classification accuracy","Image captioning performance (e.g., BLEU, CIDEr, etc. as typical for MS COCO, though not explicitly listed in the provided text)","Robustness to adversarial attacks"],"results":[{"metric":"Classification accuracy (in-domain, cross-domain, few-shot, long-tail, adversarial)","value":"TTIDA consistently outperforms other data augmentation baselines across all tested scenarios (exact numbers not provided in the excerpt)"},{"metric":"Image captioning performance","value":"TTIDA shows consistent improvements over baselines on MS COCO (exact numbers not provided in the excerpt)"},{"metric":"Robustness to adversarial attacks","value":"TTIDA enhances model robustness compared to other augmentation methods (exact numbers not provided in the excerpt)"}],"novelty_claims":["TTIDA is the first approach to combine large-scale pretrained text-to-text and text-to-image generative models for controllable and flexible data augmentation in vision tasks.","TTIDA enables the generation of photo-realistic labeled images with high diversity and controllability by conditioning T2I models on detailed descriptions from T2T models.","TTIDA is domain-agnostic and effective across various scenarios, including few-shot, long-tail, adversarial, in-domain, and cross-domain settings.","TTIDA demonstrates superior performance and robustness compared to traditional and GAN-based data augmentation methods."]}

methods: TTIDA (Text-to-Text-to-Image Data Augmentation): a pipeline that first uses a fine-tuned text-to-text (T2T) model (e.g., GPT-2, T5) to generate detailed object descriptions from label text, then uses a text-to-image (T2I) model (e.g., GLIDE) to generate photo-realistic images conditioned on these descriptions, which are used to augment the original dataset.
problems: Limited diversity and controllability in traditional and GAN-based data augmentation methods for vision tasks.
Data scarcity and imbalance in labeled datasets for computer vision tasks.
Lack of flexible and domain-agnostic data augmentation approaches for various scenarios (few-shot, long-tail, adversarial, cross-domain).
metrics: Image captioning performance (e.g., BLEU, CIDEr, etc. as typical for MS COCO, though not explicitly listed in the provided text)

results.metric: Classification accuracy (in-domain, cross-domain, few-shot, long-tail, adversarial)
results.value: TTIDA consistently outperforms other data augmentation baselines across all tested scenarios (exact numbers not provided in the excerpt)
TTIDA shows consistent improvements over baselines on MS COCO (exact numbers not provided in the excerpt)
TTIDA enhances model robustness compared to other augmentation methods (exact numbers not provided in the excerpt)

novelty_claims: TTIDA is the first approach to combine large-scale pretrained text-to-text and text-to-image generative models for controllable and flexible data augmentation in vision tasks.
TTIDA enables the generation of photo-realistic labeled images with high diversity and controllability by conditioning T2I models on detailed descriptions from T2T models.
TTIDA is domain-agnostic and effective across various scenarios, including few-shot, long-tail, adversarial, in-domain, and cross-domain settings.
TTIDA demonstrates superior performance and robustness compared to traditional and GAN-based data augmentation methods.

methods: Interventional Data Augmentation (IDA) using Text-to-Image (T2I) generators (e.g., Stable Diffusion)
Interventional prompting strategies for T2I generation
Conditioning mechanisms for T2I generation
Post-hoc filtering of generated images
Editing existing images using T2I models with interventional prompts
Synthesizing new images using T2I models with interventional prompts
Retrieving images from the T2I generator's training dataset as augmentation
problems: Performance degradation of neural image classifiers under distribution shift (non-i.i.d. conditions)
Lack of robust data augmentation methods to simulate arbitrary environmental interventions
Single Domain Generalization (SDG): Generalizing from a single source domain to unseen target domains
Reducing Reliance on Spurious Features (RRSF): Reducing classifier dependence on spurious correlations in data
datasets: A diverse collection of SDG and RRSF benchmarks (specific dataset names not provided in the excerpt)
metrics: Downstream classifier accuracy (on SDG and RRSF benchmarks)

results.metric: Downstream classifier accuracy (SDG and RRSF benchmarks)
results.value: T2I-based IDA surpasses previous state-of-the-art augmentation techniques across a broad range of benchmarks

novelty_claims: First systematic analysis of using modern T2I generators (like Stable Diffusion) for general-purpose interventional data augmentation (IDA) across diverse SDG and RRSF benchmarks.
Demonstrates that T2I-based IDA can simulate arbitrary interventions using only language prompts, without manual hard-coding or application-specific data.
Shows that T2I-based IDA outperforms previous state-of-the-art augmentation techniques regardless of configuration (prompting, conditioning, filtering).
Finds that conditioning mechanism is the most impactful factor for performance, and that post-hoc filtering is not consistently beneficial, contrary to prior work.
Compares retrieval of images from T2I generator's training data versus generation, providing new insights into their relative strengths.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Feedback-guided data synthesis framework for imbalanced classification","Diffusion model sampling strategy leveraging feedback from a pretrained classifier","Conditioning generative models on real images and label-containing text prompts","Retraining classifier on combined real and synthetic samples"],"problems":["Imbalanced classification due to long-tailed or group-imbalanced data distributions","Limited effectiveness of synthetic data augmentation due to lack of feedback from classifier to generative model","Distribution mismatch and low diversity in synthetic data generated by current generative models"],"datasets":["ImageNet-LT","Places-LT","NICO++"],"metrics":["Accuracy on underrepresented classes","Worst group accuracy"],"results":[{"metric":"Accuracy improvement on underrepresented classes (ImageNet-LT)","value":"4% increase over previous state-of-the-art, using half the amount of synthetic data"},{"metric":"Accuracy improvement on underrepresented classes (Places-LT)","value":"Nearly 4% increase over previous state-of-the-art"},{"metric":"Worst group accuracy improvement (NICO++)","value":"Over 5% increase"}],"novelty_claims":["Introduction of a feedback-guided data synthesis framework where a classifier provides feedback to the generative model to improve the usefulness of synthetic samples for imbalanced classification.","Demonstration that synthetic data must be both close to the real data distribution and sufficiently diverse for feedback to be effective.","Achievement of state-of-the-art results on multiple imbalanced datasets with greater efficiency (using fewer synthetic samples) compared to prior work."]}

Retraining classifier on combined real and synthetic samples

novelty_claims: Introduction of a feedback-guided data synthesis framework where a classifier provides feedback to the generative model to improve the usefulness of synthetic samples for imbalanced classification.

Achievement of state-of-the-art results on multiple imbalanced datasets with greater efficiency (using fewer synthetic samples) compared to prior work.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Training self-supervised visual representation learning methods on synthetic images generated by Stable Diffusion","Configuring classifier-free guidance scale in Stable Diffusion for optimal image synthesis","StableRep: A multi-positive contrastive learning method that treats multiple images generated from the same text prompt as positives for each other","Incorporating language supervision into StableRep"],"problems":["Can synthetic images generated by text-to-image models be used to learn strong visual representations?","Is it possible to match or surpass real-image-based representation learning using only synthetic data?","How to efficiently collect large-scale, diverse, and high-quality data for visual representation learning without the cost and bias of real-world data collection?"],"datasets":["CC12M","RedCaps","ImageNet"],"metrics":["Linear accuracy on ImageNet"],"results":[{"metric":"Linear accuracy on ImageNet (ViT-B/16, synthetic images only)","value":"76.7%"},{"metric":"Comparison: StableRep (20M synthetic images) vs. CLIP (50M real images)","value":"StableRep achieves better accuracy"}],"novelty_claims":["Demonstrates that self-supervised learning on synthetic images from Stable Diffusion can match or outperform learning on real images of the same sample size.","Introduces StableRep, a novel multi-positive contrastive learning approach that leverages multiple synthetic images per text prompt to promote intra-caption invariance.","Shows that with language supervision, StableRep trained on 20M synthetic images outperforms CLIP trained on 50M real images."]}

StableRep: A multi-positive contrastive learning method that treats multiple images generated from the same text prompt as positives for each other
Incorporating language supervision into StableRep
problems: Can synthetic images generated by text-to-image models be used to learn strong visual representations?
Is it possible to match or surpass real-image-based representation learning using only synthetic data?
How to efficiently collect large-scale, diverse, and high-quality data for visual representation learning without the cost and bias of real-world data collection?

results.metric: Linear accuracy on ImageNet (ViT-B/16, synthetic images only)
Comparison: StableRep (20M synthetic images) vs. CLIP (50M real images)
results.value: StableRep achieves better accuracy

novelty_claims: Demonstrates that self-supervised learning on synthetic images from Stable Diffusion can match or outperform learning on real images of the same sample size.
Introduces StableRep, a novel multi-positive contrastive learning approach that leverages multiple synthetic images per text prompt to promote intra-caption invariance.
Shows that with language supervision, StableRep trained on 20M synthetic images outperforms CLIP trained on 50M real images.

methods: Generating synthetic ImageNet clones using Stable Diffusion with class names as prompts
Class-agnostic prompt engineering to improve semantic quality and diversity of generated images

novelty_claims: First to systematically investigate replacing real images with synthetic images generated by Stable Diffusion for large-scale image classification.
Demonstrates that with minimal, class-agnostic prompt engineering, synthetic ImageNet clones can close much of the performance gap with real data for standard benchmarks.
Shows that models trained on synthetic data generalize well and perform comparably to real-data-trained models on a wide range of transfer tasks.

methods: Imagen: a text-to-image diffusion model combining a large frozen transformer language model (T5-XXL) as text encoder with a cascade of diffusion models for image generation and super-resolution
Dynamic thresholding: a new diffusion sampling technique to allow high guidance weights without degrading sample quality
Efficient U-Net: a new diffusion architecture variant that is simpler, converges faster, and is more memory efficient
Classifier-free guidance for conditioning diffusion models on text embeddings
DrawBench: a new structured benchmark for text-to-image model evaluation
problems: Generating photorealistic images from text prompts (text-to-image synthesis) with high fidelity and deep language understanding
Improving image-text alignment in generated images
Evaluating text-to-image models comprehensively across multiple semantic dimensions
Overcoming limitations of prior text encoders (e.g., CLIP) and diffusion model architectures in text-to-image synthesis

results.value: On-par with reference images
Imagen preferred over VQ-GAN+CLIP, Latent Diffusion Models, and DALL-E 2

novelty_claims: Demonstration that large frozen language models pretrained only on text (e.g., T5-XXL) are highly effective as text encoders for text-to-image generation, outperforming multimodal encoders like CLIP
Scaling the size of the frozen text encoder improves sample quality and image-text alignment more than scaling the image diffusion model
Introduction of dynamic thresholding, a new diffusion sampling technique enabling high guidance weights and improved photorealism
Proposal of Efficient U-Net, a new diffusion architecture that is simpler, faster to converge, and more memory efficient
Achievement of new state-of-the-art FID on COCO (7.27) without training on COCO
Introduction of DrawBench, a comprehensive and challenging benchmark for text-to-image evaluation

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Latent code optimization for generating more informative data from the generator","Continuous sampling from the generator during training to increase data diversity","Test time adaptation method to improve classifier accuracy on real data"],"problems":["Can classifiers be effectively trained using only generated images instead of real images?","Performance drop when training classifiers on synthetic (GAN-generated) data and testing on real images","Low diversity in randomly sampled generated images compared to real datasets"],"datasets":["ImageNet (subset)"],"metrics":["Classification accuracy","Inception Score (IS)","Frechet Inception Distance (FID)"],"results":[{"metric":"Accuracy drop (baseline, Ravuri and Vinyals [8])","value":"27.9% lower accuracy when training on generated images vs. real images"},{"metric":"Classifier performance (proposed methods)","value":"Significant improvement over naive training on randomly generated images (exact numbers not provided in excerpt)"}],"novelty_claims":["First to systematically address the problem of training classifiers solely on GAN-generated images and evaluating on real data","Propose three standalone techniques (latent code optimization, continuous sampling, test time adaptation) to improve classifier performance in this setting","Demonstrate that combining these methods can significantly reduce the performance gap between classifiers trained on real vs. generated data"]}

methods: Latent code optimization for generating more informative data from the generator
Continuous sampling from the generator during training to increase data diversity
Test time adaptation method to improve classifier accuracy on real data
problems: Can classifiers be effectively trained using only generated images instead of real images?
Performance drop when training classifiers on synthetic (GAN-generated) data and testing on real images
Low diversity in randomly sampled generated images compared to real datasets

results.value: 27.9% lower accuracy when training on generated images vs. real images
Significant improvement over naive training on randomly generated images (exact numbers not provided in excerpt)

novelty_claims: First to systematically address the problem of training classifiers solely on GAN-generated images and evaluating on real data
Propose three standalone techniques (latent code optimization, continuous sampling, test time adaptation) to improve classifier performance in this setting
Demonstrate that combining these methods can significantly reduce the performance gap between classifiers trained on real vs. generated data

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["DA-Fusion: a data augmentation strategy using text-to-image diffusion models to generate semantic variations of real images","Adapting diffusion models to new domains by inserting and fine-tuning new tokens in the text encoder for novel visual concepts","Using off-the-shelf Stable Diffusion checkpoints for image-to-image transformations"],"problems":["Lack of diversity in standard data augmentation methods, which cannot alter high-level semantic attributes in images","Improving data augmentation for few-shot image classification and real-world recognition tasks, especially with limited labeled data"],"datasets":["PascalVOC","A real-world weed recognition dataset"],"metrics":["Classification accuracy"],"results":[{"metric":"Classification accuracy improvement","value":"up to +10 percentage points over prior work"}],"novelty_claims":["Proposes DA-Fusion, a method that uses text-to-image diffusion models for semantic data augmentation, going beyond simple geometric or color transformations","Demonstrates the ability to generalize to novel visual concepts from a few labeled examples by fine-tuning the text encoder of diffusion models","Shows robustness to hyper-parameter assignment and applicability to domains outside the original diffusion model vocabulary (e.g., weed recognition)"]}

methods: DA-Fusion: a data augmentation strategy using text-to-image diffusion models to generate semantic variations of real images
Adapting diffusion models to new domains by inserting and fine-tuning new tokens in the text encoder for novel visual concepts
Using off-the-shelf Stable Diffusion checkpoints for image-to-image transformations
problems: Lack of diversity in standard data augmentation methods, which cannot alter high-level semantic attributes in images
Improving data augmentation for few-shot image classification and real-world recognition tasks, especially with limited labeled data

results.value: up to +10 percentage points over prior work

novelty_claims: Proposes DA-Fusion, a method that uses text-to-image diffusion models for semantic data augmentation, going beyond simple geometric or color transformations
Demonstrates the ability to generalize to novel visual concepts from a few labeled examples by fine-tuning the text encoder of diffusion models
Shows robustness to hyper-parameter assignment and applicability to domains outside the original diffusion model vocabulary (e.g., weed recognition)

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Explicit regularization of the generator in conditional GANs to encourage diverse outputs depending on latent codes","A simple, general regularization term that can be integrated into most cGAN objectives to control the balance between visual quality and diversity"],"problems":["Mode-collapse problem in conditional Generative Adversarial Networks (cGANs)","Lack of diversity in outputs for multi-modal conditional generation tasks","Difficulty in learning multi-modal mappings from input to output distributions in cGANs"],"datasets":["Datasets for image-to-image translation (e.g., those used in Isola et al., 2017; Wang et al., 2018; Zhu et al., 2017a)","Datasets for image inpainting (e.g., those used in Pathak et al., 2016; Iizuka et al., 2017)","Datasets for future video prediction (e.g., those used in Villegas et al., 2017)"],"metrics":["Visual quality (possibly measured by human evaluation or standard image quality metrics)","Diversity of generated outputs (possibly measured by metrics such as LPIPS or other diversity scores)"],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["Proposes a simple and general regularization method for cGANs that explicitly encourages output diversity based on latent codes","The method is widely applicable to various cGAN architectures and tasks, unlike previous approaches that are task-specific and require special architectures or objectives","The regularization allows explicit control over the trade-off between visual quality and diversity","Demonstrates that adding the proposed regularization to existing cGAN models leads to substantially more diverse generations, outperforming previous multi-modal conditional generation methods"]}

methods: Explicit regularization of the generator in conditional GANs to encourage diverse outputs depending on latent codes
A simple, general regularization term that can be integrated into most cGAN objectives to control the balance between visual quality and diversity
problems: Mode-collapse problem in conditional Generative Adversarial Networks (cGANs)
Lack of diversity in outputs for multi-modal conditional generation tasks
Difficulty in learning multi-modal mappings from input to output distributions in cGANs
datasets: Datasets for image-to-image translation (e.g., those used in Isola et al., 2017; Wang et al., 2018; Zhu et al., 2017a)
Datasets for image inpainting (e.g., those used in Pathak et al., 2016; Iizuka et al., 2017)
Datasets for future video prediction (e.g., those used in Villegas et al., 2017)
metrics: Visual quality (possibly measured by human evaluation or standard image quality metrics)
Diversity of generated outputs (possibly measured by metrics such as LPIPS or other diversity scores)
novelty_claims: Proposes a simple and general regularization method for cGANs that explicitly encourages output diversity based on latent codes
The method is widely applicable to various cGAN architectures and tasks, unlike previous approaches that are task-specific and require special architectures or objectives
The regularization allows explicit control over the trade-off between visual quality and diversity
Demonstrates that adding the proposed regularization to existing cGAN models leads to substantially more diverse generations, outperforming previous multi-modal conditional generation methods

methods: Comprehensive literature review of 36 papers on bias in T2I models
Categorization of bias into three dimensions: Gender, Skintone, Geo-Cultural
Analysis of definitions, evaluation methods, and mitigation strategies for each bias dimension
Identification of research gaps and limitations in current approaches
problems: Social bias in text-to-image (T2I) generative models
Lack of systematic review and understanding of bias in T2I systems
Scattered and inconsistent evaluation datasets and metrics for bias measurement

datasets: No specific datasets are used for evaluation in this survey; the paper reviews datasets used in prior works, but does not itself conduct new empirical evaluation
metrics: No new metrics are proposed or used in this survey; the paper reviews metrics used in prior works, but does not itself conduct new empirical evaluation
novelty_claims: First extensive survey dedicated to bias in text-to-image (T2I) generative models
Systematic categorization and review of bias dimensions (gender, skintone, geo-cultural) in T2I models
Identification of research gaps, such as under-explored geo-cultural bias and lack of studies on non-binary gender bias
Highlighting the lack of unified frameworks for bias evaluation and the limitations of current mitigation methods
Providing future research directions for human-centric bias definition, evaluation, and mitigation in T2I systems

methods: 3D Multi-Conditional Generative Adversarial Network (MCGAN) for generating synthetic 3D lung nodules on CT images
Use of two discriminators: context discriminator (real vs synthetic nodule/surrounding pairs) and nodule discriminator (real vs synthetic nodules with size/attenuation conditions)
Visual Turing Test by expert physicians to evaluate realism of synthetic images
t-SNE visualization for data distribution analysis
Training GANs without L1 loss and using a 1:1 augmentation ratio for optimal data augmentation
problems: Limited size and fragmentation of annotated medical imaging datasets, especially for 3D CT images
Lack of 3D conditional GAN-based data augmentation approaches for bounding box-based 3D object detection
Need for generating diverse and realistic synthetic nodules with varying position, size, and attenuation to improve object detection sensitivity
Reducing annotation cost for 3D object detection compared to full segmentation
datasets: Lung CT images (specific dataset name not provided in the excerpt)
metrics: Sensitivity at fixed False Positive (FP) rates

results.value: Higher sensitivity achieved under any nodule size/attenuation with MCGAN-augmented data (exact values not provided in excerpt)
Expert physicians failed to distinguish synthetic nodules from real ones

novelty_claims: First multi-conditional 3D GAN approach for generating realistic and diverse lung nodules at desired position/size/attenuation on CT images for data augmentation in object detection
First GAN-based data augmentation method applicable to any 3D object detector for boosting sensitivity at fixed FP rates with limited medical images/annotation
Demonstrates that training GANs without L1 loss and using a 1:1 augmentation ratio improves CNN-based detection performance in medical imaging

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["ALIA (Automated Language-guided Image Augmentation): automatically generates natural language domain descriptions using image captioning and LLM summarization, then uses Stable Diffusion for language-guided image editing to augment datasets; a classifier filters out low-quality or corrupted augmentations"],"problems":["Limited training data and lack of domain diversity in fine-grained classification tasks (e.g., rare animal identification)","Poor generalization of classifiers to new domains (e.g., changes in weather or location)","High cost and difficulty of collecting and labeling additional data from new domains","Limitations of existing augmentation methods: need for finetuning, reliance on user-supplied prompts, or lack of visual grounding in generated images"],"datasets":["CUB (Caltech-UCSD Birds 200)","iWildCam","Waterbirds"],"metrics":["Classification accuracy"],"results":[{"metric":"Classification accuracy improvement (iWildCam)","value":"ALIA outperforms traditional augmentation and text-to-image data by up to 7%, even surpassing the addition of real data"}],"novelty_claims":["Proposes ALIA, a fully automated pipeline for dataset augmentation that does not require finetuning of image captioning or generation models, nor user-supplied prompts","Uses LLMs to summarize image captions into domain descriptions, which are then used for language-guided image editing","Introduces a filtering step using a classifier to ensure augmented images are both diverse and preserve class-relevant information","Demonstrates that ALIA-generated data can outperform both traditional augmentation and text-to-image generation, and even surpasses the addition of real data in some cases"]}

methods: ALIA (Automated Language-guided Image Augmentation): automatically generates natural language domain descriptions using image captioning and LLM summarization, then uses Stable Diffusion for language-guided image editing to augment datasets; a classifier filters out low-quality or corrupted augmentations
problems: Limited training data and lack of domain diversity in fine-grained classification tasks (e.g., rare animal identification)
Poor generalization of classifiers to new domains (e.g., changes in weather or location)
High cost and difficulty of collecting and labeling additional data from new domains
Limitations of existing augmentation methods: need for finetuning, reliance on user-supplied prompts, or lack of visual grounding in generated images

results.value: ALIA outperforms traditional augmentation and text-to-image data by up to 7%, even surpassing the addition of real data

novelty_claims: Proposes ALIA, a fully automated pipeline for dataset augmentation that does not require finetuning of image captioning or generation models, nor user-supplied prompts
Uses LLMs to summarize image captions into domain descriptions, which are then used for language-guided image editing
Introduces a filtering step using a classifier to ensure augmented images are both diverse and preserve class-relevant information
Demonstrates that ALIA-generated data can outperform both traditional augmentation and text-to-image generation, and even surpasses the addition of real data in some cases

Generation of synthetic datasets using Conditional Deep Convolutional Generative Adversarial Network (DCGAN)
Traditional data augmentation techniques for dataset expansion
